title: Photographer

rationales.taskAutomation: Stock and product photography, plus batch retouching, are largely reproducible by generative image tools; event, portrait, and photojournalism work still require physical presence and human rapport.
rationales.toolMaturity: Generative image tools (Midjourney, Firefly) and AI editing suites (Lightroom AI, Luminar) are mature and standard in the workflow.
rationales.adoption: E-commerce and stock-photo buyers have shifted much of their volume to AI generation; event and portrait segments show far less adoption.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agent pipelines can shoot-list, batch-edit, and tag product catalogs end-to-end; they cannot replace a photographer's physical presence at a shoot.

tasksAtRisk: Stock photography production for common subjects
Batch photo editing, retouching, and background removal
Photo metadata, tagging, and catalog organization
tasksGrowing: Event and portrait photography requiring physical presence
Creative direction and art direction for shoots
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 27-4021 Photographers, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Generative image tools have largely displaced commodity stock and product photography. Photographers whose work depends on being physically present — weddings, events, editorial shoots — remain far more insulated than catalog and stock work.
